name: Priya — The Organized Barcelona Traveler
summary: All booking ingestion, data retrieval, AI context awareness, ExtractBookingField, recommend_booking, FTC disclosure, and itinerary integration tests passed. The AI accurately referenced specific booking details (terminals, check-in times, tour times, meeting points) from ingested data. No data integrity issues detected.

ux_issues.description: Day 5 has a scheduling conflict: both the Picasso Museum Visit and a full-day Girona & Dali Museum tour are listed at order_in_day=1. The AI acknowledged the conflict in its response text and offered resolution options, but the itinerary data itself still contains the double-booking. Ideally the AI should resolve the conflict before saving, or at minimum not assign both items to the same order position.
ux_issues.context: Day 5 items: Picasso Museum (order 1, sightseeing), Girona & Dali (order 1, activity), Quimet & Quimet lunch (order 2, meal)

ux_issues.description: On the day trip recommendation response, the AI called recommend_booking twice. The first call worked correctly and the response was complete. The second call at the end of the response resulted in a 'no response from AI' error. The user would see the complete response but the error event at the end is unnecessary.
ux_issues.context: Second recommend_booking call with query 'Girona and Dali Museum day trip from Barcelona with transport and tickets' returned tool result but AI produced no follow-up, triggering error event.

ux_issues.description: El Xampanyet tapas recommendation on Day 4 mentions 'Try the house cava' which is an alcoholic beverage. Priya explicitly stated she doesn't drink much. While cava is culturally relevant to Barcelona, the AI should have noted a non-alcoholic alternative or avoided leading with an alcohol recommendation for this user.
ux_issues.context: Day 4 item: 'Tapas in El Born: El Xampanyet - Try the house cava and the anchoas'. This was in the initial itinerary built before Priya stated her alcohol preference.

tool_failures.description: Second invocation of recommend_booking in the same turn produced a tool result but the AI generated no follow-up response, causing a 'no response from AI' error event. The primary response was already complete, so this is a minor issue.

ftc_disclosure_check.details: The AI included 'Affiliate disclosure: I may earn a small commission if you book through this link.' in its response after recommend_booking returned affiliate links. Disclosure was present and clearly visible.

trip_creation.details: create_trip tool invoked correctly in selection mode. Trip 'Barcelona Getaway' created with destination_country ES and status PLANNING.

flight.details: All flight details correctly parsed: airline, flight number, terminals, seat, cabin class, passenger name.

hotel.details: All hotel details correctly parsed: name, dates, room type, address, phone, number of guests.

activity.details: All activity details correctly parsed: name, date, time, duration, location, operator, price, number of guests, meeting notes.

departure_terminal_via_chat.details: AI correctly answered 'Terminal 4 at JFK' from ingested flight booking data.

hotel_checkin_via_chat.details: AI correctly answered '3:00 PM' check-in at Hotel Arts Barcelona.

activity_time_via_chat.details: AI correctly answered with date, time, and meeting point details.

extract_booking_field_checkin.details: ExtractBookingField correctly extracted check_in_time=3:00 PM, check_in_date=2026-06-15.

extract_booking_field_meeting_point.actual: Nativity Facade entrance (Carrer de la Marina side)
extract_booking_field_meeting_point.details: ExtractBookingField correctly extracted meeting point and guide identifier (orange Barcelona Expert Tours sign).

triggered_for_bookable_item.details: recommend_booking correctly triggered when user asked for a day trip with transport and tickets included.

not_triggered_for_restaurant.details: recommend_booking was NOT triggered when user asked for restaurant recommendations. AI used create_itinerary_items instead.

ftc_disclosure_included.details: Affiliate disclosure statement present in AI response after recommend_booking results.

itinerary_integration.details: 6-day itinerary built with bookings correctly placed: Day 1 flight, Day 2 hotel check-in, Day 3 Sagrada Familia tour, Day 4 Montserrat day trip, Day 5 Girona day trip, Day 6 checkout. The AI proactively created itinerary items incorporating the booked activities at correct times.
itinerary_integration.issues: Day 5 has a scheduling conflict (Picasso Museum + full-day Girona tour at same order position). AI acknowledged conflict in text but did not resolve it in the data.

usefulness_scores.persona_handoff: N/A — not tested in this persona
usefulness_scores.companion_mode: N/A — not tested in this persona

notes: Booking ingestion pipeline is solid — all three booking types (flight, hotel, activity) parsed accurately with structured fields.
AI context awareness is excellent — the AI answered specific questions about terminals, check-in times, and tour details correctly from ingested booking data without hallucinating.
ExtractBookingField RPC works reliably and extracts structured key-value pairs from booking raw text.
FTC affiliate disclosure is properly included when recommend_booking is used.
The AI correctly avoided triggering recommend_booking for simple restaurant recommendations.

The AI respected the no-alcohol preference when explicitly stated in the restaurant request, but the earlier auto-generated itinerary included a cava recommendation (before the preference was stated).
Minor double-booking conflict on Day 5 could be improved — the AI should resolve conflicts in the itinerary data, not just acknowledge them in text.
